channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Burnley want Bellamy after announcing Parker exit
item.description: Burnley are interested in appointing Craig Bellamy as manager after Scott Parker leaves following relegation from the Premier League.

item.title: 'I lost myself' - Bright on retirement, abuse and the future
item.description: Former England and Chelsea captain Millie Bright speaks to BBC Sport a day after announcing her retirement.

item.title: Mainoo signs new Man Utd deal until 2031
item.description: Kobbie Mainoo signs a new contract with Manchester United that will keep him at Old Trafford until 2031.

item.title: Former England defender Young to retire aged 40
item.description: Ashley Young will end his playing career at Ipswich after saying it will be "23 years and out" at the end of this season.

item.title: Morrison named West Brom boss on permanent basis
item.description: After keeping West Bromwich Albion in the Championship this season, James Morrison is given the head coach job on a permanent basis.

item.title: When Wrexham's first Hollywood season ended in final-game tears
item.description: As Wrexham aim for the Championship play-offs, ex-player Paul Rutherford recalls it hasn't all been promotion success for Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac.

item.title: Were Arsenal right to be 'fuming' with refereeing after Atletico draw?
item.description: Mikel Arteta was left "fuming" with the officials after Arsenal's draw at Atletico Madrid - what did they get right and wrong?

item.title: 'Too much to handle' - Bright on social media abuse
item.description: Millie Bright sits down with the BBC's Jo Currie to talk about her decision to retire from football.

item.title: Pochettino sad to see Spurs suffering
item.description: Tottenham's Premier League relegation struggles are "difficult to accept", says former manager Mauricio Pochettino.

item.title: England captain Williamson signs new Arsenal deal
item.description: Leah Williamson is the fourth player to commit her future to the club in recent weeks, with Kim Little, Stina Blackstenius, and Steph Catley also signing new deals.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Ella and Jake from Jamie Johnson FC
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on Ella and Jake from BBC football drama Jamie Johnson FC plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's fixtures.

item.title: Captain Thiago and triple Arsenal - FPL team of the week
item.description: With four weeks left in the FPL season, it is about striking a balance between finding explosive differentials and keeping faith in the consistent performers.

item.title: Man Utd consider Leao move - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United considering move for Rafael Leao, Newcastle eye new goalkeeper as part of summer rebuild and Arsenal still keen on Julian Alvarez.

item.title: England to leave Tottenham in summer
item.description: Tottenham captain Bethany England can leave "feeling proud" of what she has achieved at the club, says managing director Andy Rogers.

item.title: Stiles' son 'disgusted' over FA brain injury claim
item.description: Campaigners are disgusted the FA has apparently denied a link between heading the ball and brain injuries.

item.title: 'We lost our home, not our identity' - Shakhtar eye European glory
item.description: Shakhtar Donetsk go into their Uefa Conference League semi-final tie against Crystal Palace on Thursday hoping to make their country proud.

item.title: Music & methods - why Forest could be Europa League favourites
item.description: A recent revival has lifted Nottingham Forest's hopes as they chase their Europa League dream against Aston Villa.

item.title: VAR denies Arsenal late penalty in draw against Atletico
item.description: Viktor Gyokeres puts Arsenal ahead from the spot before an equalising penalty from Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez in the second half. A third penalty call for the Gunners is overturned by VAR which ensures the first leg of their Champions League semi-final ends in a 1-1 draw.

item.title: Arteta 'fuming' at overturned Eze penalty
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta believes that the penalty awarded against Atletico Madrid for a foul on Eberechi Eze was not a "clear and obvious error", meaning VAR did not need to intervene and the original on-field decision to give the penalty should have stood.

item.title: Inspiring being part of Arsenal squad - Blackstenius
item.description: Forward Stina Blackstenius talks about Arsenal's Women's Champions League hopes and explains why she has extended her stay with the club.

item.title: PSG edge out Bayern in first-leg nine-goal classic
item.description: Ousmane Dembele and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia both score twice to give PSG a 5-4 lead in their Champions League semi-final tie against Bayern Munich, after a pulsating first leg at the Parc Des Princes.

item.title: Arsenal hit seven to seal Leicester play-off fate
item.description: Arsenal close the gap on leaders Manchester City to eight points with two games in hand, after a 7-0 win over Leicester City - who will finish bottom of the Women's Super League.

item.title: VAR controversially overturns Arsenal penalty
item.description: Watch the moment an Arsenal penalty for a foul on Eberechi Eze by Atletico Madrid's David Hancko is overturned by VAR in their Champions League first-leg tie.

item.title: Should VAR have intervened to overrule Arsenal penalty?
item.description: BBC Sport pundits Stephen Warnock, Nedum Onuoha and Rory Smith debate whether VAR was right to overturn a decision to award Arsenal a penalty against Atletico Madrid for a foul on Eberechi Eze.

item.title: Who has the better front three - Bayern or PSG?
item.description: BBC Sport experts Stephen Warnock and Nedum Onuoha analyse the difference between the attacking trios of Bayern Munich and PSG in their Champions League semi-final first leg.

item.title: Parker's final post-match interview as Burnley boss
item.description: Burnley head coach Scott Parker says his side must "come back stronger" after their 1-0 defeat to Manchester City meant they were relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: All I want to do is help as much as I can - Grealish
item.description: England midfielder Jack Grealish speaks to Joe Wilson about extending his Special Olympics GB ambassador role and what it means to him.

item.title: Chelsea's Mudryk appeals against four-year FA ban
item.description: Chelsea winger Mykhailo Mudryk takes his fight against a Football Association drugs ban to the Court of Arbitration for Sport.

item.title: Man Utd can win Premier League next season - Mount
item.description: Manchester United's Mason Mount feels the club can end their 13-year wait for a Premier League title next season.

item.title: Skinner confident he is right person for Man Utd job
item.description: Manager Marc Skinner says he has "no doubts" that he remains the best man for the job at Manchester United, despite criticism from fans.

item.title: Arsenal 'hunt' Man City - what 7-0 win means for WSL title race
item.description: Arsenal have made up ground on Manchester City, but can they actually win the Women's Super League title this season?

item.title: Zaragoza keeper banned for 13 games for punching rival
item.description: Real Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada is banned for 13 matches after punching a Huesca player in the face.

item.title: Goalkeeper, 70, set to feature for Spanish club
item.description: When he pulls on the gloves for CD Colunga against CD Praviano in the fifth tier of Spanish football on Sunday, Angel Mateos Gonzalez will become the oldest player to appear in an official match in Spain.

item.title: Liverpool expect Salah return before end of season
item.description: Liverpool expect Mohamed Salah to return from injury before the end of the season after he was forced off against Crystal Palace on Saturday.

item.title: Are Celtic & Hearts fans really rooting for their rivals?
item.description: With Hearts out in front, Hibs could do the the Scottish Premiership leaders a favour against Celtic, while the defending champions will hope Rangers can do some damage at Tynecastle.

item.title: Howson to make history as first black FA Cup final official
item.description: Akil Howson will become the first black official to feature in an FA Cup final when Chelsea take on Manchester City next month.

item.title: PSG's Hakimi out of Bayern return leg with injury
item.description: Paris St‑Germain full-back Achraf Hakimi has been ruled out for the "next few weeks" after suffering an injury during their dramatic win against Bayern Munich in the Champions League.

item.title: The Europa League winner turned director, actor, DJ and photographer
item.description: Alfie Whiteman picked up a Europa League winner's medal last year, now at 27 he has retired from football and is opening his first photography exhibition.

item.title: 'Hungry' Wrexham to monitor results during Boro test
item.description: Phil Parkinson will have to watch games elsewhere in the Championship as they look to secure a top six spot on Saturday.

item.title: How to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend
item.description: BBC Sport Ask Me Anything explains how to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend.

item.title: Bright reflects on 'incredible' 2022 Euros win
item.description: Speaking in 2022, Chelsea captain Millie Bright reflected on the "best summer of my life" and England's "incredible" Women's Euros victory.

item.title: Man Utd survive Brentford fightback to earn crucial win
item.description: Manchester United hold their nerve as first-half goals from Casemiro and Benjamin Sesko are enough for them to win an entertaining encounter with Brentford.

item.title: Carrick hails 'big three points' after beating Brentford
item.description: Manchester United interim boss Michael Carrick says that his side's goal is "to finish as high up the league as possible" following their 2-1 win against Brentford at Old Trafford.

item.title: Andrews 'bitterly disappointed' after Brentford lose at Man Utd
item.description: Brentford boss Keith Andrews says his side aren't "playing fearful" football and that he doesn't want to be that sort of team after a 2-1 loss to Manchester United in the Premier League.

item.title: Fernandez fires Chelsea to FA Cup final showdown with Man City
item.description: Enzo Fernandez' first-half header gives Chelsea a 1-0 win over Leeds United in the semi-final of the FA Cup at Wembley Stadium.

item.title: Lingard on playing in Brazil and Man Utd
item.description: Jesse Lingard talks about moving to Corinthians, and discusses why Michael Carrick should get the Manchester United manager's job.

item.title: How lack of goalline technology denied Kerr hat-trick
item.description: Women's Football Show pundits Gilly Flaherty and Nia Jones look at Sam Kerr's legitimate goal that wasn't given due to a lack of goalline technology in the WSL.

item.title: Why Mourinho could be Real's 'ultimate wildcard'
item.description: With Real Madrid out of the Champions League, and falling behind in La Liga, noise around manager Alvaro Arbeloa's future is unsurprisingly increasing. Could Jose Mourinho be the answer?

item.title: A record-breaking semi-final - the antidote to modern football?
item.description: A scintillating semi-final first leg between Paris St-Germain and Bayern Munich left many to wonder if this was the antidote to modern football.

item.title: Afghanistan women can return to competition
item.description: Afghanistan women will become a "symbol of resilience" after Fifa approved their return to international competition, says former captain Khalida Popal.

item.title: Who's next for Premier League relegation?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the Premier League relegation battle.

item.title: White House wants Department of Homeland Security to reopen for World Cup
item.description: White House officials preparing for this summer's World Cup urge the Department of Homeland Security to reopen after an attempted assassination of US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
item.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

item.title: The remarkable rise of Chelsea's little-known interim boss
item.description: Chelsea's interim head coach Calum McFarlane admits this year has been a "whirlwind", going from obscurity to an FA Cup semi‑final at Wembley.

item.title: How 10 Premier League teams can qualify for Europe
item.description: Ten Premier League teams in Europe? It sounds preposterous, but it is not as far-fetched as it seems.

item.title: Why Rashford must be patient over Barcelona future
item.description: Marcus Rashford is not ready to give up on his Barcelona dream just yet, writes Spanish football expert Guillem Balague.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: World Cup 2026 injury watch - key names racing to be ready
item.description: After Spain's Lamine Yamal was ruled out of the rest of his club season, BBC Sport looks at other key names racing to be fit for the World Cup - and those who are set to miss out through injury.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: Ross County relegated again? Or will Airdrie or Morton face drop?
item.description: It's relegation judgement night on the final Friday of the Scottish Championship season proper, with Morton, Airdrieonians and Ross County all at risk of being sucked into League 1 before the night is out.

item.title: Saving Livingston would be career high, says Bartley
item.description: Manager Marvin Bartley says keeping Livingston in the Scottish Premiership this season would rank as the finest achievement of his career.

item.title: McKenna keen to reward Clarke loyalty at World Cup
item.description: Scott McKenna hopes to reward Scotland coach Steve Clarke's "loyalty" and help the national team make history at this summer's World Cup.

item.title: McCoist urges Rangers to sign Premier League striker - gossip
item.description: Rangers hero urges Ibrox club to sign Premier League striker as Motherwell star addresses reported transfer interest from Celtic...

item.title: McInnes, McGlynn & Naysmith vie for manager award - but Askou misses out
item.description: Falkirk's John McGlynn is in the running for a third successive PFA Scotland manager of the year award, facing competition from Derek McInnes of Hearts and Stenhousemuir boss Gary Naysmith.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: What's on the line as WSL reaches business end?
item.description: BBC Sport analyses the state of play in the Women's Super League with only a handful of games to go and the title, European qualification, relegation and promotion still to be decided.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
